name: Santuari del Miracle to Bassa de l'Alzina Trail
name_original: Balsa de Alzina desde Santuario del Miracle
description: Hike 4.7 miles (7.6 km) from Santuario del Miracle to Balsa de Alzina, a moderate route with 635 feet (193 metres) elevation gain.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle traverse a landscape characterized by natural water features and varied terrain. The region includes forested areas and offers a mix of gentle paths and moderate ascents. Hikers can explore streams and the waterfalls that define these routes, providing a setting for outdoor activity. The area's natural environment supports a range of outdoor pursuits. Best waterfall hiking trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle The most popular waterfall hiking route is Fuente de la Mina – Solsona loop from Solsona, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route features gentle elevation changes through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Solsona – Plaça de Sant Joan, Solsona loop from Solsona, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail offers accessible terrain with minimal elevation gain, suitable for easy waterfall walks. Local hikers also love the Carles Morató Library – Fuente de la Mina loop from Solsona, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate ascents, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Hiking in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle is defined by natural water features, forested paths, and gentle to moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy waterfall walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: There are 14 waterfall hiking trails documented in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy strolls to more moderate hikes through the region's natural water features.

Q: Are there easy waterfall walks suitable for beginners in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: Yes, Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle offers several easy waterfall walks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina – Solsona loop from Solsona&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail with gentle elevation changes, perfect for a relaxed outing. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10474701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona – Plaça de Sant Joan, Solsona loop from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;, also an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path.

Q: What is the typical duration for waterfall trails in this region?
A: The duration of waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle varies. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina – Solsona loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Longer, moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10474987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carles Morató Library – Fuente de la Mina loop from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 3 hours 40 minutes to finish.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: Many of the waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mina – Plaça Major de Solsona loop from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40313778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Manel – Fuente de la Mina loop from Solsona&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.1-mile (4.9 km) option.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: The best time to visit the waterfalls in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle is typically during spring or after periods of significant rainfall. This ensures the waterfalls are at their most impressive, with a strong flow of water. Autumn can also be beautiful, with vibrant foliage adding to the scenic experience.

Q: What should I wear for waterfall hiking in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: For waterfall hiking, it's advisable to wear comfortable, layered clothing suitable for varying temperatures. Sturdy, waterproof hiking boots with good grip are essential, as trails near waterfalls can be damp and slippery. Consider bringing a waterproof jacket, even on clear days, due to potential spray from the falls.

Q: Is wild swimming permitted or possible at any of the waterfalls?
A: While the region features natural water features and waterfalls, specific regulations regarding wild swimming can vary. It's always recommended to check local signage or official regional guidelines before attempting to swim in natural bodies of water to ensure safety and compliance with environmental protection rules.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: The waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene natural environment, the refreshing presence of the waterfalls, and the well-maintained paths that make these routes enjoyable for various skill levels.

Q: Are the waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many of the waterfall trails in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle are suitable for families. There are several easy routes with minimal elevation gain that are perfect for children. These paths allow families to enjoy the natural beauty and the waterfalls without overly strenuous effort.

Q: Are there any hidden gem waterfall hikes in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle that are less crowded?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, focusing on trails slightly off the most popular paths or visiting during off-peak hours can lead to a more secluded experience. Many of the region's 14 trails offer a chance to connect with nature away from larger crowds, especially those that are moderate in difficulty.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of waterfall hikes in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle?
A: The waterfall hikes in Espai D'Interès Natural Del Miracle range from easy to moderate. Out of the 14 available routes, 5 are classified as easy, featuring gentle terrain and minimal elevation changes. The remaining 9 routes are moderate, involving slightly longer distances or more significant ascents, offering a good challenge for those looking for a bit more exertion.
tips: This sanctuary dates from the 17th century and is located in the Solsonès region. Of strong Marian devotion, it was built following the apparition of the Virgin to two shepherds in the year 1458.
The Alzina pond has viewpoints to observe the fauna and flora of the natural space.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins at the Santuario del Miracle. You'll find ample parking facilities available directly at the sanctuary, making it convenient to start your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Balsa de Alzina from Santuario del Miracle trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered relatively easy and is ideal for beginners and families with children. It features mild difficulty and low elevation gain, making it a pleasant experience for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, including footpaths, trails, and some minor road sections. You'll walk through dry oak groves, small pine forests, and cultivated drylands. While generally well-maintained, some segments might have loose terrain, so sturdy footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery. You'll start at the historic Sanctuary of El Miracle, pass through extensive cereal fields, and enjoy views of the Sierra del Verd and Pedraforca mountains. The highlight is the Balsa de Alzina, an artificial reservoir with a bird observatory, surrounded by rich natural habitats.

faqs.question: Is the Balsa de Alzina from Santuario del Miracle trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Espai d'Interès Natural del Miracle, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Espai d'Interès Natural del Miracle?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail within the Espai d'Interès Natural del Miracle. The area is freely accessible for visitors to enjoy its natural beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for most of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with wildflowers in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter offers a quieter experience, though some sections might be cooler.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route follows the GR 7, a long-distance footpath. It also intersects with sections of other official trails such as E07 and El Camí IT2TR3 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat.

faqs.question: Is the Balsa de Alzina from Santuario del Miracle a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This specific route is an out-and-back trail. You will follow the same path to the Balsa de Alzina and then return to the Santuario del Miracle.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific facilities directly on the trail itself, you will find restrooms and potentially water sources at the starting point, the Santuario del Miracle. It's always recommended to carry enough water for your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see, especially at Balsa de Alzina?
faqs.answer: The Balsa de Alzina is a protected natural space and features an aquatic bird observatory, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching. You might observe various aquatic species and amphibians, as well as common woodland birds and small mammals in the surrounding forests.
